title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a deterministic queue-filter query suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.
Use IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and NULLIF only when their business meaning is justified.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Where Predicates · changed case · Quiz Where Predicates
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 2, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
